justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Prototype pollution identified by name as the primary critical finding, (2) __proto__ key attack vector explicitly explained with `for (const key in source)` iteration mechanism, (3) Global Object.prototype impact clearly stated ('polluting Object.prototype for the ENTIRE process, every object in memory now has isAdmin === true'), (4) Safe merge fix recommended (guard __proto__/constructor/prototype keys, or replace with explicit field picking), (5) Specific attack payload provided (curl command with {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), (6) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Textbook-quality prototype pollution explanation — names the vulnerability, shows the exact iteration mechanism, and traces the full attack chain from request to global state corruption
Excellent concrete reproduction curl command that any developer can immediately test
Fix recommendation covers both the minimal guard (skip __proto__/constructor/prototype) and the better architectural fix (explicit field picking + validation library)
Goes beyond the rubric by identifying two additional high-severity issues: mass assignment and array/falsy corruption in deepMerge — both legitimate and well-explained
Correctly notes Object.hasOwn as an alternative to for...in to avoid inherited property traversal
Accurately describes the isAdmin escalation scenario matching the expected behavior exactly
